How to Tolerate Working With Obnoxious People

Working with obnoxious people can make the work environment so uncomfortable that you are less productive. You may find yourself dreading each day, feeling stressed, or getting angry. However, with the proper tools, you can make it through the day and remain professional. It is important to remember that the obnoxious person is a reflection of the company and not yourself. You can change how you are treated by changing jobs, but in the meantime, you can learn how to deal with the situation.

Changing Your Attitude

You may be able to change your attitude by looking at the situation from the other person's perspective. Consider what motivates them and what they are going through in their personal life. This can help you feel more compassion toward them, which can make it easier to work with them.

Focus on Your Work

If you find that the obnoxious person is distracting you, try to focus on your work. This may mean putting in headphones or finding a quiet place to work. You can also try to work with the person less if possible.

Talking to a Manager

If the obnoxious person is making it impossible for you to work, talk to a manager. Explain the situation and what you are experiencing. The manager may be able to help resolve the issue.

Let $X$ be an equivariant embedding of a connected reductive group $G$ over
an algebraically closed field $k$ of positive characteristic. Let $B$ denote a
Borel subgroup of $G$. A $G$-Schubert variety in $X$ is a subvariety of the
form $\diag(G) \cdot V$, where $V$ is a $B \times B$-orbit closure in $X$. In
the case where $X$ is the wonderful compactification of a group of adjoint
type, the $G$-Schubert varieties are the closures of Lusztig's $G$-stable
pieces. We prove that $X$ admits a Frobenius splitting which is compatible with
all $G$-Schubert varieties. Moreover, when $X$ is smooth, projective and
toroidal, then any $G$-Schubert variety in $X$ admits a stable Frobenius
splitting along an ample divisors. Although this indicates that $G$-Schubert
varieties have nice singularities we present an example of a non-normal
$G$-Schubert variety in the wonderful compactification of a group of type
$G_2$. Finally we also extend the Frobenius splitting results to the more
general class of $\mathcal R$-Schubert varieties.

Chris Pascoe (born in London, England) is the lead vocalist and songwriter of rock band The Stranglers.
Pascoe was born on 8 May 1951 to parents who were both professional musicians; his father played trumpet while mother sang opera.  He grew up listening to music from all over the world including American soul music such as James Brown & Aretha Franklin which he listened to at home via radio broadcasts or records bought by his parents.   His first musical experience came when aged four years old singing along to songs sung by his mother's operatic friends during their rehearsals.    At age eight Chris began playing guitar after receiving one as Christmas present from his grandfather.   By eleven years old he had formed his own group called "The Blackbirds" where they performed covers of popular bands like Led Zeppelin, Deep Purple and Rolling Stones amongst others.   In 1967 he joined another local band called "Spike Jones" before joining the "Strangers" later that year.   During this time he also attended art school studying painting but left without completing it due to lack of interest.   After leaving art college he worked briefly as a waiter/barman until being offered work as a roadie for the Stranglers' touring crew.   This led him into meeting Hugh Cornwell aka Bill Wyman whom he met through mutual friend Paul Raymond.   It wasn't long before he became involved in writing lyrics for some of the band's early singles.   As well as working full-time as a member of the Stranglers he continued to write poetry throughout most of 1970s and 1980s.   He has been married twice - firstly to singer/songwriter Sue Moran between 1974-1979 then again since 1988 to artist/musician Linda Perry.   He currently resides near Paris France

Не мисля, че ситуацията след изборите е неприятна, защото този път имаме по-малко фрагментация на Народното събрание в сравнение с предишното такова, когато в него имаше осем партии, а създаването на работеща коалиция беше наистина трудно. Тогава коалицията беше много трудно създадена, състоеше се от много субекти – 4 на брой, а менажирането й се оказа трудна задача. Това каза в предаването „В развитие“ с водещ Делян Петришки политологът и преподавател в Софийския университет Стойчо Стоев. Темата в минутите след 12:00 – възможните политически конфигурации в следващото управляващо мнозинство. „В настоящия парламент се оформя едно ясно дясно мнозинство около ГЕРБ, „патриотите“ и Марешки. Патриотите най-вероятно ще бъдат официализирани във властта. Този път се съмнявам те да участват само с подкрепа, по скоро ще искат да се включат с поемането на някои от ресорите. Чисто математически, коалиция само между ГЕРБ и „патриотите“ е възможна, но аз съм почти убеден, в нея ще се включи и „Воля“ на Веселин Марешки, защото, първо, 122 депутати са крехко мнозинство, и второ, коалиция с два субекта е малко по-проблемна от гледна точка на това, че всеки един конфликт би могъл да доведе до нейния разпад“, заяви гостът в студиото. „Говорейки за традиционните десните партии, които не успяха да влязат в Народното събрание, може да кажем, че парламентът ще бъде лишен от реформаторско настроени хора по отношение на съдебната система и антикорупцията. Действително „Да, България“ и до голяма степен ДСБ бяха ангажирани с тези политики. От парламентарно представените партии сега няма такава, чиито приоритет да бъде ясно изразена съдебна реформа“, допълни още политологът. Очаквайте видео с целия разговор по темата, както и коментар по най-„горещото“ външнополитическо събитие от днешния ден – задействането на чл. 50 от Лисабонския договор от страна на британския премиер Тереза Мей и ефектите от това върху Европа и България.

In this work, we investigate the critical mass of bacterial populations in a generalized Keller-Segel model and draw an analogy with Chandrasekhar's limiting mass of white dwarf stars. The development of biofilms is an important biological process in which bacteria self-organize and move towards nutrient sources. Keller-Segel models have been widely used as a framework to study this process.

We derive analytical expressions for the critical mass of bacterial populations and explore its dependence on various model parameters. Our results suggest that the critical mass depends sensitively on the diffusion coefficients of the chemical attractants and repellents, as well as the bacterial motility. We also investigate the impact of model complexity, such as introducing logistic growth terms, on the critical mass.

The problem this work attempts to solve is understanding the conditions under which bacterial populations can form biofilms. Our approach is motivated by the analogy with Chandrasekhar's limiting mass, which is a crucial concept in astrophysics that describes the maximum mass a white dwarf star can sustain before it collapses. Our key idea is that the critical mass acts as a threshold for the stability of bacterial populations, beyond which biofilms can form.

The methodology we employ involves a combination of analytical and numerical techniques, including linear stability analysis, scaling arguments, and numerical simulations. Our analysis provides insight into the dynamics of bacterial populations and has practical implications for the development of antibacterial strategies.

If you're installing cabling inside a pre-built house then there may already be some wires installed.  You don't necessarily have to follow those paths but if they exist, they're probably easier than trying to create new ones yourself.   The easiest way to install cables in such cases is by using what is called "staggered patch panels" which allow you to route multiple types of cables along different routes without having to worry about them crossing over each other.    This method also allows you to easily change the path later when needed.     In order... Continue reading →

The most important part here is planning ahead!   Decide exactly where you want the network to go -  who wants internet? who wants phone lines?  Who does not care?  What type of equipment do you plan to connect to the network?    How many devices do you expect to plug-in simultaneously?    Do you anticipate adding more rooms and/or floors in the future?    These questions must be answered prior to beginning installation because laying out the entire system beforehand makes things far simpler during actual implementation.    Once you've decided upon a layout, mark every outlet/device that requires connection onto a large sheet of paper.  Then draw arrows between outlets indicating the direction you'd like data flow to occur.  Remember that you cannot simply lay CAT 5e/6/7 anywhere; instead, you must ensure that the wire runs parallel to the ground plane wherever possible.  Otherwise, signal loss could result due to impedance mismatch....

There is disagreement among headache researchers about where primary headache pain signals originate. One faction theorizes the pain signals originate in the meninges, the three layers of membranes between the brain and the inside of the skull. Another faction argues the pain signals originate in the brainstem.

Me, I believe headache pain originates right where it hurts.

I believe migraine pain originates mainly in and around the array of major sensory apparatus of the head: the retinas, olfactory bulbs, vestibular apparatus and semicircular canals, dorsal surface of the tongue, and tooth pulp. That would readily explain the typical locations of migraine pain as well as the sensory disturbances associated with migraine.

The most common location of migraine pain, for example, is around and behind the eyes. That's the location of the retinas of the eyes, which are packed with dense concentrations of neurons (nerve cells). I believe that during a migraine episode those many neurons emit adenosine, a neurotransmitter chemical, and the resulting concentration of released adenosine causes migraine head pain and other migraine symptoms.

Adenosine causes pain by binding to (briefly attaching to) adenosine receptors embedded in the outside membranes of neurons.

لاہور 92نیوز فاسٹ باولر محمد عامر ایک بار پھر لارڈز میں ٹیسٹ کھیلنے کےلئے تیار ہیں دنیاکی نگاہیں ان پرلگی ہیں وہ پہلی بال کیسی پھینکیں گے اس پر کروڑوں کا سٹہ لگ گیا ہے تفصیلات کے مطابق لارڈز کے تاریخی کرکٹ گراونڈ پر محمد عامر کی یہی وہ نوبال تھی جس نے ان پر کرکٹ کے دروازے بند کر دیے اور ذلت رسوائیوں کے دروازے کھول دیے اب فاسٹ باولر نے پانچ برس بعد ایک بار پھر ٹیسٹ کرکٹ کی دنیا میں قدم رکھ دیا ہے میدان ایک بار پھر وہی لارڈز کا ہے جہاں پاکستان اور انگلینڈ کل 14 جولائی کو منے سامنے ہوں گے انگلینڈ کی بلے بازی کے دوران جب گیند عامر کے ہاتھ میں ہو گی تو ہر ذہن میں ایک ہی سوال ہو گا فاسٹ باولر اپنی پہلی گیند کیسی پھینکے گا قابل ذکر بات یہ ہے کہ اس پر کروڑوں کا سٹہ لگ گیا ہے کہ عامر اپنی پہلی گیند وائیڈ پھینکے گا یا نو یہی نہیں اب تو عامر کی ہر گیند ہی دنیا تنقید کی عینک لگا کر دیکھے گی محمد عامر کی صلاحیتیں کسی سے ڈھکی چھپی نہیں اور وہ دنیا کی کسی بھی بیٹنگ لائن کو اکیلے تہس نہس کر سکتے ہیں اسی لئے تو انگلش میڈیا نے عامر کو دباو میں لانے کےلئے ان پر تنقید کے تیروں کی بارش کر دی ہے لیکن عامر کے ٹیلنٹ کے قدردانوں کی بھی دنیا میں کمی نہیں پہلے کوہلی ان کی تعریف کر چکے اب ویسٹ انڈیز کے سابق جارحانہ اوپنر ڈیسمنڈ ہینز کا کہنا ہے کہ عامر اتنے اچھے باولر ہیں کہ اگر وہ مستقل مزاجی کےساتھ کھیلتے رہے تو سابق عظیم فاسٹ باولر اینڈی رابرٹس کی طرح اپنے دامن میں کامیابیاں سمیٹ سکتے ہیں

When it comes to seeing stars in our peripheral vision, it all comes down to the makeup of our eyes themselves. While our pupils are capable of contracting and dilating to let in more or less light, our eyes are generally optimized for looking at objects directly in front of us. When we look directly at a star, the bright light can be overwhelming and cause an effect known as "visual adaptation." Essentially, our eyes adjust to the brightness of the star, making it more difficult to distinguish from the blackness of space.

However, when we look at a star out of the corner of our eye, it falls on a part of our retina called the "peripheral retina." This part of our eye is much better at detecting differences in brightness and contrast, so stars that might be too dim or faint to see when looking directly at them can be easily picked up by the peripheral retina. Additionally, since our peripheral vision is better at detecting motion, a star might become more noticeable when it appears to be moving across the sky or flashing.

Overall, the combination of our eyes' physical makeup and the way that they are optimized for perceiving different types of light and motion help explain why we can sometimes see stars more clearly in our peripheral vision than when we look directly at them. It's just one of the many fascinating quirks of the way that our eyes and brains work!

Convergence of measures is a topic in measure theory that studies convergence criteria and trends between measures on similar spaces. This field of study is important as it provides insights into the relationship between different types of measures, and can be used to develop powerful models for comparison. The most general approach to convergence of measures is the Radon-Nikodym theorem, which states that if two measures have the same sets of masses, then their values must be equal for those sets. This theorem can be used to determine the set of mass measures for which convergence is assured. It can also be used to understand how closely related two measures are and to predict the rate of convergence. In modern mathematics, convergence of measures is further developed using the notion of weak convergence. This approach looks at the behavior of a sequence of measures over some space and looks at how they converge over time. Weak convergence can be used to prove weak versions of the Radon-Nikodym theorem and has been applied in a range of fields, including probability theory and harmonic analysis. Another approach to the topic of convergence of measures is the notion of absolute continuity. This looks at how two measures can be made to coincide by introducing a third measure that is absolutely continuous with each of them. This approach can be used to compare densities and Lebesgue integrals and it can also be used to prove versions of the Radon-Nikodym theorem. The relationship between measures can also be studied using the notion of bounded variation. This provides an upper bound on the difference between measures, which can be used to prove their mutual absolute continuity. This has been used to study the relationship between probability measures and the notion of convergence for certain processes, such as Markov chains. Finally, measure theory has been used to study the generalized notion of convergence of measures. This allows for a unified approach to the theory of convergence of measures, as it unifies a great deal of different criteria and trends into a single framework. As such, convergence of measures is an important topic in measure theory and has been used in a wide range of applications.

Jakarta, CNN Indonesia -- Anggota DPR dari Partai Gerindra Fahri Hamzah membuat pernyataan mengejutkan saat mengunjungi lokasi pembangunan Light Rail Transit (LRT) di Jakarta Selatan. Fahri mengungkapkan kecurigaannya terhadap pemerintah terkait pembuatan tiang LRT yang terlalu tinggi. "Saya curiga, kenapa bikin tiang LRT tinggi-tinggi? Ini kan bahaya, kalau angin kencang bisa tumbang," ujar Fahri kepada wartawan, Rabu (15/7). Menurut Fahri, tiang LRT yang dibangun tinggi-tinggi itu justru menimbulkan ancaman bagi keamanan pengguna LRT dan masyarakat sekitar. Dia menyebutkan, jika tiang tersebut tumbang, maka akan sangat membahayakan kehidupan manusia. "Saat ini kan sedang musim angin, ntar kalau angin kencang bisa tumbang tiang LRT ini. Apalagi di sekitar sini kan banyak sekolah, kalau tumbang bisa menimbulkan korban jiwa," kata Fahri. Dia menduga alasan dibuatnya tiang LRT terlalu tinggi itu dilakukan untuk memudahkan kontraktor dalam pekerjaannya. Namun, hal itu menurut Fahri, seharusnya tidak boleh dijadikan alasan mengorbankan keselamatan pengguna LRT dan masyarakat sekitar. "Saya dengar yang bikin LRT ini anaknya menteri, ya mungkin karena kerjaan kontraktor jadi dipermudah. Saya lihat ini kok dibuat tinggi banget, jangan-jangan ada kepentingan tertentu di balik ini," ujar Fahri. Fahri kemudian meminta kepada pihak yang bertanggung jawab untuk segera meninjau kembali pembuatan tiang LRT tersebut. Dia berharap ada tindakan cepat dan tanggap dari pihak pemerintah dalam mengatasi persoalan ini. "Saya minta ke pihak terkait untuk meninjau ulang pembangunan ini. Jangan sampai nanti seperti Monas yang tiba-tiba tumbang karena tiangnya dipangkas," tutur Fahri. Hingga saat ini, belum ada komentar resmi dari pemerintah maupun kontraktor LRT terkait pernyataan Fahri ini. Namun, publik punya hak untuk mempertanyakan keselamatan pembangunan infrastruktur seperti LRT. Maka, diharapkan pihak terkait dapat memberikan penjelasan yang jelas dan meyakinkan.

The main idea of this paper is that the generator is trained to produce contrastive samples with minimal energies, while the discriminator is trained to assign high energies to these generated samples. Viewing the discriminator as an energy function allows to use a wide variety of architectures and loss functional. Among them, we show one instantiation of EBGAN framework as using an auto-encoder architecture, with the energy being the reconstruction error. The main strength of this paper is that it views the discriminator as an energy function, which allows to use a wide variety of architectures and loss functional. In particular, we show that this form of EBGAN exhibits more stable behavior than regular GANs during training. We also show that a single-scale architecture can be trained to generate high-resolution images. The weakness is that the main idea is based on the energy, which is abstract, making it difficult to interpret the inner working. In summary, this is an interesting and relevant paper that demonstrates the application of energy-based methods for GANs, and introduces a potentially interesting framework. The main idea is promising, and the approach might be applied in other GAN variants. Overall, this paper is worth a read for anyone interested in generative adversarial networks, energy-based methods for GANs, or just generative modeling in general! Kishore K. Dalmia

SERANG – Sindikat pencurian dan pemberatan (curat) diringkus tim gabungan Reserse Mobile (Resmob) Polda Banten, Reskrim Polres Serang dan Serang Kota. Mereka ditangkap  setelah beraksi di rumah salah seorang anggota jajaran Polda Banten di Perumahan Kaujon Mansion, Kota Serang. Tak hanya menggondol barang berharga, kawanan maling spesialis rumah warga inipun juga membawa kabur senjata api pistol milik personil yang bertugas di Satuan Sabhara tersebut. Mereka yang ditangkap, Angga (24) warga Kelurahan Karundang, Kota Serang, Hendra (23)  warga Kelurahan Cipocok Jaya, Kota Serang, Nandi (28), Khaerudin (25)  Mahmudin. Mereka semua  warga Kelurahan Tembong, Kota Serang. Tersangka Hendra terpaksa dilumpuhkan dengan timah panas karena melakukan perlawanan. Selain mengamankan pelaku, turut diamankan sejumlah barang bukti seperti pistol dinas, satu unit telpon genggam dan dua bilah senjata tajam. Diperoleh keterangan, peristiwa pencurian ini terjadi Kamis (22/6/2018) sekira pukul 04.00 WIB. Aiptu DS yang saat itu berada di rumah hanya dengan keponakannya, awalnya tidak memilik kecurigaan apapun. Sebab, sampai pukul 03.00WIB, ia masih terjaga usai menonton salah satu pertandingan sepak bola piala dunia. “Habis  nonton bola saya langsung istirahat di kamar. Pas bangun mau solat subuh, saya kaget liat jendela belakang kok kebuka tapi pintu depan masih dikunci. Terus ngecek tas berisi pistol sama 2 handphone, udah enggak ada,” ungkap DS kepada wartawan, Senin (25/86/2018). Setelah mengalami kejadian ini, korban kemudian melaporkan insiden tersebut ke Mapolres Serang Kota. Usai melapor, kasus itu kata DS, berhasil diungkap pihak kepolisian tidak lebih dari 24 jam. “Pelakunya juga sekarang sudah ditahan di Polres,” ucapnya. Kapolres Serang Kota, AKBP Komarudin membenarkan kasus tersebut. Satu orang pelaku kata Kapolres, terpaksa dilumpuhkan dengan timah panas lantaran melakukan perlawanan saat hendak diamankan petugas “Para pekaku sudah kita amankan dan masih menjalani pemeriksaan petugas reskrim,” katanya. (haryono/tri)

The authors present Adversarial Multi-Task Learning (AMTL) for text classification.
They use neural networks trained jointly across multiple related tasks using AMTL.  They report improved performance over single-task training methods when evaluated against several benchmark data sets. 
Their results suggest that it is possible to learn useful generalizations about language without explicitly modeling linguistic phenomena such as syntax and semantics. This may make them easier to apply than more linguistically sophisticated techniques like deep recurrent nets. Their work also suggests ways to improve transferability between languages. Strengths: 
1. Improved accuracy compared to single-task training methods; 2. Easy-to-apply technique; 3. Transferable knowledge Weaknesses: 
1. No comparison made to state-of-the-art systems based on RNNs

It could, if we fed the war machine like we did in WW2.

People talk about the military industrial complex and how much we spend on defense, but it is honestly nothing compared to a nation actually going to war collectively. We could, if necessity arose, raise the investment in the military by 1000%+. Heck, WW2 saw a military that was 8x as large as we have now... with a lot less national population to draw from.

Imagine almost everyone quit their "civilian" jobs tomorrow and either joined the military or worked in a military support job (factory, etc). Imagine they took most of their paychecks and lent them to the government (via bonds). Imagine they took their belongings and melted them down for war material. Instead of pots and pans being turned into tanks it might be cellphones scrapped for heavy metals. When there's an existential threat to your way of life, people can throw their lives into war.

We could make a lot more war machines then, no?


tl;Dr - Not a priority. Probably won't be unless we get invaded by aliens.

ڈیوائن جونسن، جو ہالی ووڈ کے اداکاروں میں شامل ہیں، دنیا کے سب سے زیادہ کمانے والے اداکار ہیں۔ ان کی کمائی کے بارے میں مختلف معلومات دستیاب ہیں، لیکن ان کی کمائی کے بارے میں کچھ واضح نہیں ہے۔

جونسن کو ۲۰۲۰ میں فوربس کی فہرست میں دنیا کے سب سے زیادہ کمانے والے اداکار کے طور پر شامل کیا گیا تھا۔ ان کی کمائی اس سال تقریباً ۹۳ ملین ڈالر تھی۔

جونسن کی کمائی کے بارے میں دیگر معلومات بھی دستیاب ہیں۔ ان کے پاس ایک خصوصی جیت جیت کے لئے ہے، جس کی قیمت تقریباً ۱۰ ملین ڈالر ہے۔ ان کے پاس ایک بہت بڑی پراپرٹی پورٹفولیو بھی ہے، جس میں ہاؤسز، فلیٹس، کمرشل پراپرٹیز اور دیگر املاک شامل ہیں۔

جونسن کے پاس ایک بہت بڑی کار کلیکشن بھی ہے، جس میں لمبی لمبی کاروں کے علاوہ ہیچ بیمر، فیراری، لمبورگینی، مرسڈیز بینز اور دیگر برنڈز کی گاڑیاں شامل ہیں۔

جونسن کے پاس ایک بہت بڑی فلیٹ بھی ہے، جو لس اینجلس کے ایک بہت بڑے علاقے میں واقع ہے۔ اس فلیٹ کی قیمت تقریباً ۲۷ ملین ڈالر ہے۔

جونسن کی کمائی کے بارے میں کچھ واضح نہیں ہے، لیکن ان کے پاس بہت سارے ذرائعِ معاش بھی ہیں۔ ان کے پاس ایک بہت بڑا سپانسرشپ بھی ہے، جس کے لئے وہ مختلف برانڈز کے لئے اشتہارات کرتے ہیں۔

جونسن کی کمائی کے بارے میں کچھ واضح نہیں ہے، لیکن ان کی مالی حیثیت کے بارے میں کچھ معلومات دستیاب ہیں۔ ان کی مالی حیثیت تقریباً ۳۰۰ ملین ڈالر ہے۔

The following tips will help reduce bacteria levels on surfaces where food is prepared.  These steps can also be used when preparing meals outside the home.   The CDC recommends these practices because they have been shown effective against many common pathogens associated with foodborne illnesses.    Wash your hands before handling any type of food, especially raw meats such as chicken, beef, pork, lamb, veal, turkey, ham, sausage, salami, bacon, etc..    Wear disposable gloves while cleaning up spills from raw meat products so that no germs get into contact with uncooked areas of... Continue reading →

Clean Your Hands Before Cooking Food  Always wear rubber gloves whenever you're working around raw meat.     Wash your hands often during meal preparation.        Don't touch anything that's not food unless you've washed your hands first.      Never prepare food without wearing protective clothing.       Do not eat or drink near food prep area.         Remove jewelry before preparing food.        Cover cuts and wounds with bandages before preparing food.        Throw away old toothbrushes.

It has to do with one-to-one correspondences. So for example, with every natural number we can relate exactly one even number, therefore we say the set of naturals and the set of even numbers have the same "cardinality". This is true even thought the set of even numbers is a "proper subset" of the natural numbers.

It can be shown, however, that there exists *no* one-to-one correspondence between the naturals and the real numbers, therefore their cardinalities differ. And also with every natural number you can relate *at least* one real number (namely the number itself, e.g. 0 with 0, 1 with 1, etc.), so we say that the set of real numbers has greater cardinality than the set of natural numbers. What it isn't true is that you can relate every real number with a natural number.

To get a better understanding I suggest you learn what an "injection", a "surjection", and a "bijection" is. (A bijection is a function which is in a one-to-one correspondence. And also you can show a function is a bijection if and only if it is a surjection and an injection.)

In the U.S.A. there are about 6 million people in jail. This is about one-sixth of the total population. About half of all people in jail are black, even though black people make up about 12 percent of the population. Many people in jail have not been convicted of any crime. According to the British Medical Journal, nearly 6 percent of the U.S. population has a relative in jail. In the 1800s and 1900s, the U.S. prison system grew dramatically. At the start of the 1900s, there were about 200,000 people in U.S. prisons. Since then, the numbers have grown dramatically. The U.S. prison population is about 300,000 more than it was just 10 years ago. The number of people in prison has outpaced the number of people being born in the U.S. every year since the 1960s. Many people believe that the U.S. criminal justice system is too harsh and that there are too many people in jail. There are many different reasons that America has this many people in jail. One reason is that many people are poor and cannot afford good legal representation. If someone does not have money, it is much harder to get the police and the courts to listen to what they have to say. It also costs a lot of money to stay in jail. Each night in jail costs about $30. This means that it is cheaper to keep someone in jail than it is to pay for their legal defense. This is true for people who are not rich. Even if someone has a lawyer, it is very expensive. As a result, many poor people who get in trouble with the law get sent to jail. Some people think that the American criminal justice system focuses too much on punishment and not enough on rehabilitation. The government wants to send a message to people who break the law. However, jail is not a good place to teach someone a lesson. Instead, rehabilitation programs in jail can teach prisoners positive ways to think and act. If someone has been in jail for different periods of time, it is very difficult to tell how long they should be there. Most judges do not use sentences in jail for longer than a year. If someone has a job while they are in jail, their sentence is likely to be shorter. The U.S. criminal justice system says that "justice is revenge". This means that the American government wants to send a message to everyone by putting very harsh punishments on criminals. If a person hurts someone else, the government wants the person who was hurt to get revenge. In theory, this makes sense. However, it does not work in practice. This means that not very many people are talked into volunteering to help fix problems between people. Instead, people talk about punishment and revenge, which makes people fear the law and not want to get involved. This means that there are still many

The definition of the amplituhedron in terms of sign flips involves both
one-loop constraints and the "mutual positivity" constraint. To gain an
understanding of the all-loop integrand of $\mathcal{N}=4$ sYM requires
understanding the crucial role played by mutual positivity. This paper is an
attempt towards developing a procedure to introduce the complexities of mutual
positivity in a systematic and controlled manner. As the first step in this
procedure, we trivialize these constraints and understand the geometry
underlying the remaining constraints to all loops and multiplicities. We
present a host of configurations which correspond to various faces of the
amplituhedron. The results we derive are valid at all multiplicities and loop
orders for the maximally helicity violating (MHV) configurations. These include
detailed derivations for the results in arXiv:1810.08208 [hep-th]. We conclude
by indicating how one might move beyond trivial mutual positivity by presenting
a series of configuration which re-introduce it bit by bit.

In a character driven book, your character's wants and needs should be of upmost importance. Oftentimes, the primary action of a character driven book involves how a character's wants, needs, and opinions change over time. As you begin to carve out the basics of your book, spend some considering what your characters want.All characters should yearn for something. This can help drive their actions throughout a story. For example, maybe your main character desperately wants to be a professional ballet dancer. His passion for dance can be the driving point of his actions throughout the story.
Your characters should, on a deeper level, need something as well. Maybe your main character's desire to dance is driven by a deeper need for control. Ballet requires a certain amount of perfection. If your character has had a messy life, for example, he may use dance as a way to create perfection from chaos.;
, In a character driven book, interpersonal and internal changes are what mark the trajectory of your story. No one wants to read about a character who stays stagnant. Your story arch is built around your characters' inner journeys. Decide going into the writing process how your characters are going to change.Your main character's wants and needs should change in response to what happens around him or her. Think of all the things that can change a person over the course of a lifetime. Relationships can change us. Jobs can change us. Trauma and loss can also foster change. Current events can alter a character's worldview.
Think about how certain events will shape your character. Many character driven books, for example, are driven by a relationship. Maybe your ballet dancer can reconnect with his estranged father. He may become less focused on success as he mends some issues from his past. His big realization may be that his career aspirations are unrealistic or too rigid, and they stem from his own troubled past.
When in doubt, return to your theme. Think about the topics you want to explore. You can shape character development and events around your theme. If you're writing a story of redemption, for example, how a character feels about him or herself will be a focal point focal point of your story. What events could occur to shift your character's opinion of him or herself? How will your character change based on these events?

, Even when a story is character driven, there is always a plot. It may not be action and adventure based, but you should have some plot in mind before beginning your story. Character driven stories are often focused on personal journeys. Having a literal journey, like a road trip, can help drive a symbolic one. You could also focus on how certain relationships change. If you're telling the story of a friendship, a romance, or a family relationship, how will this relationship progress? What events will occur to drive this relationship forward?

, Oftentimes, a setting is very important in a character driven book. The relationship characters have to their home is often a big aspect of their identity. Decide where your story will take place and, from there, spend some time thinking about the relationship your characters have with that place. Do they love the setting or do they hate it? Are they longing for escape, or looking for a way to settle down in their current location?

, Every book has a theme. This the central idea or message that the story explores. Figuring out your theme, or having some idea of what topics you want to explore, can help you develop your characters. The theme your work grapples with should be of central importance to the characters.Think about your favorite book. If you had to sum that book up with a few words, what would those words be? For example, say your favorite book is Mrs. Dalloway. What is the first word you think of when considering this book? Words that come to mind may be time, death, trauma, and loss. These are certainly all themes explored in Mrs. Dalloway, primarily through the book's characters.
Think about a theme you want to explore in your book. Do you want to write about love, loss, heartbreak? Do you want to write a story of redemption or courage? Try to jot down a variety of themes you would like to explore in your book. Think about how your characters could exemplify those themes.

Етнографският музей на София ще организира изложение с акцент върху музейското старение на българските традиции. Зад идеята на изложението е да представя на потребителите исторически, културни и духовни ценности. Проектът "Стрйок" разказва история за това дали да се поставят предизвикателствата пред България през последните десетилетия. Главното откритие пред тази промяна е иновативността, адаптацията и духа за перфекционизъм - три духовни носители на нашите културни традиции.

"Стройко" ще представи изложение с различни видове експонати. Разгледателите ще могат да видят индивидуално работени дрехи и дамски предгости, музикални инструменти, изработки от лен и кожа, както и много други традиционни културни останки. Ще бъдат разказвани истории за костюмите, аксесоарите и неизчезващият дух на коледарство. Ще бъдат заснети изложби на работниците от различните професии, градове и села в България, както и, само за тази изложба, работениците от BulFoto.

BulFoto ще зарадва настаняващите със своята студио на място. Тази студио ще предлага възможност за характеризирана фотография на всеки разгледател с декор в предназначено за това място. Фотографията по желание на посетителя ще им бъде предавана в скенер.

Изложбата е предназначена да представя българските културни характеристики и духа на възпитанието към традициите. Друг акцент за изложбата ще бъде поставен и на творческото самопроявяне по време на проекта. Очаква се да присъстват и начинание и инициативи от различни автори като ученици, студенти и майстори на областта. На изложбата ще бъдат представени ученици от школи и/или специалности, ще се провеждат срещи и дискусии между авторите и с разработчиците. В рамките на изложбата има възможност за участие на студенти и дипломирани специалисти, направили работи за реализацията на изложбата и предварително одобрили ваучери.

Eтнографският музей с проекта "Стройко" ще даде възможност да разясним и съзнаем ценността на нашата култура. Ще стимулираме предложения за неща, които да подценят и трансформират нашата традиционна култура, а също и целите и идеите да ни предизвикат да окажем правилно портрета на основното интелектуално нисъко на България. Стройко ще даде още една възможност да търсим това нещо допълнително, което да изразява нашата българска душа.

We reflect on the fact that our understanding of time is closely related to our concepts of beginning and end, and raise questions about whether the beginning and end of time can be conceived as real events in the history of the universe. We review the history of the idea of the beginning of time, and of the corresponding idea that time will end. We also consider different conceptions of time, including the role of science, and the importance of the direction of time. In particular, we focus on the idea that time is a dimension, and on the idea that the dimension of time is relative to the observer. We then pose the question whether the beginning and end of time can be conceived as events in the history of the universe, and explore possible answers to this question. In particular, we focus on the role of the observer in the conception of time. We conclude that, although the beginning and end of time can be conceived as events in the history of the universe, they cannot be defined unambiguously, and that the question whether time has a beginning and an end is well-posed only if time is conceived as an absolute, independent of the observer.

This paper addresses the problem of how to assess the readability of Vietnamese text. The paper presents a new formula for calculating the readability of Vietnamese text, and it is tested on a sample of texts from various sources.

Strengths: The paper presents a new formula that takes into account the unique characteristics of Vietnamese text. The formula is tested on a sample of texts from various sources, and it is shown to be a reliable measure of readability.

Weaknesses: The paper does not present a comparison of the new formula with other measures of readability, so it is not clear how well the new formula performs in comparison to other measures. Additionally, the paper does not discuss the limitations of the new formula, so it is not clear how well the new formula performs in certain situations.

Set aside a definite budget. Consider your transportation needs. Shortlist cars. Analyze the information in the ad. Contact the seller at a reasonable hour. During this phone call verify the make, model, year, mileage, vehicle identification number and general condition of the vehicle. Schedule an appointment to see the actual car and take it for a spin. Ask for a maintenance history for the vehicle. Use the VIN to do a detailed car history search. Make a thorough inspection of the vehicle, with the engine off and on. Inspect the vehicle for any signs of water damage. Inspect the car for any rust or body damage. Examine the mileage on the vehicle. Check the tires, especially the front, for wear. Examine the battery. Check the air filter. Examine the engine’s coolant and oil levels while the car is off. Check the transmission fluid with the engine running. Test drive the car. Make sure the seller has all the necessary paperwork. Have a professional mechanic inspect the car and perform any necessary tests at your expense as mechanics tend to favor the person paying for the inspection. Make an offer if the car suits you. Get the signed title from the seller.

The paper is well-written and logical in its approach. I do, however, have a few concerns. First, this is a distributed lock-in system. Can you provide some assurance that all parties will be using the same space, i.e., not linkable? Second, it only captures a single view of the world. How do you ensure that all views will converge? Third, will this system be susceptible to "winner's curse" and not be able to escape from its local optimum? Mills' peer review comments are in-line with the above comments and some others. For the distributed lock-in system, we apply fully-gossip-based algorithm to ensure all parties in the system are using the same space (and thus are linkable). We also apply consistent hashing to ensure all views would converge. As for the winner's curse, we will also apply normalization trick to reduce the possibility. We will put the implementation details in the paper. We believe these are very reasonable concerns and the system will achieve the goal of building a single unified semantic space for linkable, but random PPMI-enabled data. describe this system as "linkable", but not "linkable linkable". What does this mean? It means that the data is semantically homogeneous, but the connection between the data is not explicit or abstract, which means you can't get from one to the other, and the two groups of data cannot be "linked". For example, the space is "linkable" but not "gender-linkable" because a person's gender can be inferred by the name, but in the generated space, two names with the same "inference" are actually totally different. Another example is the movie rating data. A user may rate a movie with 4 stars and 4 stars are "linkable" to each other, but "linkable" does not mean that rating 1 and rating 2 actually represent the same movie, they can represent totally different movies. In fact, this is a key feature of the generated space, because if two totally different and random data point the same place, they will get aggregated and compress the data in that area, thus making the model stronger.

TEMPO.CO, Jakarta - Kepala Staf Kepresidenan Moeldoko meminta para calon kepala daerah dan tim pemenangannya tidak mengangkat isu yang berbau suku, ras, agama, dan antargolongan (SARA) dalam pemilihan kepala daerah atau pilkada. "Jangan coba-coba mengangkat isu SARA yang memunculkan pertikaian dan konflik," kata Moeldoko di kantornya, Jakarta, Senin, 25 Juni 2018. Moeldoko mengatakan isu SARA sangat sensitif. Topik tersebut dapat menjadi sentimen negatif dalam pertarungan pilkada. Karena itu, Mantan Panglima Tentara Nasional Indonesia tersebut meminta semua calon kepala daerah menggunakan cara yang santun. Simak: Apa Gebrakan Moeldoko di KSP? Tak hanya kepada para peserta pemilu, dia juga meminta negarawan berpikir lebih jernih dan bijaksana dalam mengelola isu SARA. Dia memahami topik tersebut tampak menarik untuk diangkat, terlebih guna membenturkan sesuatu. "Tapi janganlah isu SARA digunakan. Karena begitu memasuki area itu, sesungguhnya nanti pada akhirnya akan memukul dirinya sendiri," ujarnya. Moeldoko mengajak semua pihak saling mengingatkan soal isu SARA. "Kami meminta kesadaran bersama untuk berpikir kepentingan yang jauh lebih besar, bukan kepentingan pragmatis," ucapnya. Apa Kata Moeldoko Soal Pelibatan TNI dalam Isu Teroris? Pilkada serentak akan digelar pada 27 Juni 2018. Sebanyak 171 daerah di 17 provinsi ikut serta dalam pemilihan tersebut. Moeldoko berharap gelaran itu lancar. Namun, jika provokasi dari isu SARA memunculkan situasi yang tidak stabil, Moeldoko memastikan aparat keamanan akan bekerja.

This paper studies the problem of having mobile robots in a multi-robot
system maintain an estimate of the relative position and relative orientation
of near-by robots in the environment. This problem is studied in the context of
large swarms of simple robots which are capable of measuring only the distance
to near-by robots.
  We present two distributed localization algorithms with different trade-offs
between their computational complexity and their coordination requirements. The
first algorithm does not require the robots to coordinate their motion. It
relies on a non-linear least squares based strategy to allow robots to compute
the relative pose of near-by robots. The second algorithm borrows tools from
distributed computing theory to coordinate which robots must remain stationary
and which robots are allowed to move. This coordination allows the robots to
use standard trilateration techniques to compute the relative pose of near-by
robots. Both algorithms are analyzed theoretically and validated through
simulations.

By using the Green Bank Telescope to measure neutral hydrogen absorption against XTE J1810-197, an X-ray pulsar, we have found that the distance to this magnetar is 3.5(+/-0.5) kpc, in agreement with estimated distances determined from its dispersion measure and X-ray spectra. We have determined a lower limit of $N_{\rm H} \ga 4.6 \times 10^{21}$ cm$^{-2}$ for the non-ionized hydrogen column density toward \magnetar, which is also consistent with X-ray spectra estimates. It is likely that XTE J1810-197 is located near an infrared dark cloud known as G10.74-0.13.

Zirconium dioxide () (sometimes known as zirconia, not to be confused with zircon) is a white crystalline oxide of zirconium. Its most naturally occurring form, with a monoclinic crystalline structure, is the mineral baddeleyite. A dopant stabilized cubic structured zirconia, cubic zirconia, is synthesized in various colours for use as a gemstone and a diamond simulant.

Production, chemical properties, occurrence
Zirconia is produced by calcining zirconium compounds, exploiting its high thermostability.

Structure
Three phases are known: monoclinic below 1170 °C, tetragonal between 1170 °C and 2370 °C, and cubic above 2370 °C.  The trend is for higher symmetry at higher temperatures, as is usually the case. A small percentage of the oxides of calcium or yttrium stabilize in the cubic phase.  The very rare mineral tazheranite, , is cubic. Unlike , which features six-coordinated titanium in all phases, monoclinic zirconia consists of seven-coordinated zirconium centres. This difference is attributed to the larger size of the zirconium atom relative to the titanium atom.

Chemical reactions
Zirconia is chemically unreactive.  It is slowly attacked by concentrated hydrofluoric acid and sulfuric acid.  When heated with carbon, it converts to zirconium carbide.  When heated with carbon in the presence of chlorine, it converts to zirconium(IV) chloride.  This conversion is the basis for the purification of zirconium metal and is analogous to the Kroll process.

Engineering properties 

Zirconium dioxide is one of the most studied ceramic materials.  adopts a monoclinic crystal structure at room temperature and transitions to tetragonal and cubic at higher temperatures. The change of volume caused by the structure transitions from tetragonal to monoclinic to cubic induces large stresses, causing it to crack upon cooling from high temperatures. When the zirconia is blended with some other oxides, the tetragonal and/or cubic phases are stabilized. Effective dopants include magnesium oxide (MgO), yttrium oxide (, yttria), calcium oxide (), and cerium(III) oxide ().

Zirconia is often more useful in its phase 'stabilized' state. Upon heating, zirconia undergoes disruptive phase changes. By adding small percentages of yttria, these phase changes are eliminated, and the resulting material has superior thermal, mechanical, and electrical properties. In some cases, the tetragonal phase can be metastable. If sufficient quantities of the metastable tetragonal phase is present, then an applied stress, magnified by the stress concentration at a crack tip, can cause the tetragonal phase to convert to monoclinic, with the associated volume expansion. This phase transformation can then put the crack into compression, retarding its growth, and enhancing the fracture toughness. This mechanism, known as transformation toughening, significantly extends the reliability and lifetime of products made with stabilized zirconia.

The  band gap is dependent on the phase (cubic, tetragonal, monoclinic, or amorphous) and preparation methods, with typical estimates from 5–7 eV.

A special case of zirconia is that of tetragonal zirconia polycrystal, or TZP, which is indicative of polycrystalline zirconia composed of only the metastable tetragonal phase.

Uses
The main use of zirconia is in the production of hard ceramics, such as in dentistry, with other uses including as a protective coating on particles of titanium dioxide pigments,  as a refractory material, in insulation, abrasives, and enamels.

Stabilized zirconia is used in oxygen sensors and fuel cell membranes because it has the ability to allow oxygen ions to move freely through the crystal structure at high temperatures. This high ionic conductivity (and a low electronic conductivity) makes it one of the most useful electroceramics. Zirconium dioxide is also used as the solid electrolyte in electrochromic devices.

Zirconia is a precursor to the electroceramic lead zirconate titanate (PZT), which is a high-κ dielectric, which is found in myriad components.

Niche uses
The very low thermal conductivity of cubic phase of zirconia also has led to its use as a thermal barrier coating, or TBC, in jet and diesel engines to allow operation at higher temperatures. Thermodynamically, the higher the operation temperature of an engine, the greater the possible efficiency. Another low-thermal-conductivity use is as a ceramic fiber insulation for crystal growth furnaces, fuel-cell stacks, and infrared heating systems.

This material is also used in dentistry in the manufacture of subframes for the construction of dental restorations such as crowns and bridges, which are then veneered with a conventional feldspathic porcelain for aesthetic reasons, or of strong, extremely durable dental prostheses constructed entirely from monolithic zirconia, with limited but constantly improving aesthetics. Zirconia stabilized with yttria (yttrium oxide), known as yttria-stabilized zirconia, can be used as a strong base material in some full ceramic crown restorations.

Transformation-toughened zirconia is used to make ceramic knives. Because of the hardness, ceramic-edged cutlery stays sharp longer than steel edged products.

Due to its infusibility and brilliant luminosity when incandescent, it was used as an ingredient of sticks for limelight.

Zirconia has been proposed to electrolyze carbon monoxide and oxygen from the atmosphere of Mars to provide both fuel and oxidizer that could be used as a store of chemical energy for use with surface transportation on Mars. Carbon monoxide/oxygen engines  have been suggested for early surface transportation use, as both carbon monoxide and oxygen can be straightforwardly produced by zirconia electrolysis without requiring use of any of the Martian water resources to obtain hydrogen, which would be needed for the production of methane or any hydrogen-based fuels.

Zirconia can be used as photocatalyst since its high band gap (~ 5 eV) allows the generation of high energetic electrons and holes. Some studies demonstrated the activity of doped zirconia (in order to increase visible light absorption) in degrading organic compounds and reducing Cr(VI) from wastewaters.

Zirconia is also a potential high-κ dielectric material with potential applications as an insulator in transistors.

Zirconia is also employed in the deposition of optical coatings; it is a high-index material usable from the near-UV to the mid-IR, due to its low absorption in this spectral region. In such applications, it is typically deposited by PVD.

In jewelry making, some watch cases are advertised as being "black zirconium oxide". In 2015 Omega released a fully  watch named "The Dark Side of The Moon" with ceramic case, bezel, pushers, and clasp, advertising it as four times harder than stainless steel and therefore much more resistant to scratches during everyday use.

In gas tungsten arc welding, tungsten electrodes containing 1% zirconium oxide (a.k.a. zirconia) instead of 2% thorium have good arc starting and current capacity, and are not radioactive.

Diamond simulant

Single crystals of the cubic phase of zirconia are commonly used as diamond simulant in jewellery. Like diamond, cubic zirconia has a cubic crystal structure and a high index of refraction. Visually discerning a good quality cubic zirconia gem from a diamond is difficult, and most jewellers will have a thermal conductivity tester to identify cubic zirconia by its low thermal conductivity (diamond is a very good thermal conductor). This state of zirconia is commonly called cubic zirconia, CZ, or zircon by jewellers, but the last name is not chemically accurate. Zircon is actually the mineral name for naturally occurring zirconium(IV) silicate ().

How to Tell when a Girl Is Interested in You

If you're wondering if a girl likes you, then this article is for you. You'll get tips on how to tell if a girl is interested in you, including when a girl is hinting that she likes you and when a girl is interested in you but not ready to admit it.
This article also includes information on how to make a girl like you.

How to Tell when a Girl Is Interested in You
There are many ways to tell if a girl is interested in you. For example, you can tell if a girl likes you by the way she looks at you, the way she talks to you, and the way she acts around you. You can also tell if a girl likes you by the things she does for you and the way she makes you feel.

If a girl likes you, she will often look at you in a way that is different from how she looks at other people. She may seem to be staring at you, even when she doesn't speak to you. This is called eye contact, and it can be a powerful signal that she likes you.

When a girl likes you, she may also talk to you differently than she talks to other people. She may speak to you in a softer voice, or she may be more flirtatious. She may also ask questions about you or your life, which is another sign that she likes you.

If a girl likes you, she may also act differently around you. She may be more nervous or excited when she's around you. She may also be more willing to do things for you or go out of her way to help you.

Finally, if a girl likes you, she may make you feel different than other people. She may make you feel special or important. She may also be more affectionate or intimate with you than she is with other people.

The body's ability to limitlessly produce certain cell types is a great mystery that scientists are constantly trying to unravel. The way cells are produced and differentiated within the body is incredibly complex and fascinating.

During development, cells gradually differentiate into more and more specific types, forming organs and tissues. At the same time, stem cells, which are undifferentiated cells, replenish and restore cells that have become damaged or worn out. Different organs and tissues produce their own types of stem cells, and these stem cells are responsible for producing different types of cells within that particular organ or tissue.

So why can't the body make more vital cell types such as brain cells? It is because certain organs or tissues don't produce the particular type of stem cell needed to make that particular cell. This means that these organs don't have a built-in source of regeneration, and so they cannot replace any cells that have been damaged or worn out. This can be a problem, as we are constantly losing brain cells as we age, and so their number gradually decreases.

On the other hand, the body can almost infinitely produce less vital cell types such as body hair. This is because the body's ability to produce these cells is governed by what is known as homeostasis. This is the process by which the body maintains its balance and produces just enough of a particular type of cell. So while the body may produce an abundance of body hair cells, it only produces a specific number that it needs to maintain balance.

In conclusion, the limits that our bodies have when producing certain cell types is very complex and fascinating. The ability to limitlessly produce certain types of cells depends on a variety of factors, including the availability of specific stem cells and the body's ability to maintain homeostasis.

Real-world programs are neither monolithic nor static -- they are constructed
using platform and third party libraries, and both programs and libraries
continuously evolve in response to change pressure. In case of the Java
language, rules defined in the Java Language and Java Virtual Machine
Specifications define when library evolution is safe. These rules distinguish
between three types of compatibility - binary, source and behavioural. We claim
that some of these rules are counter intuitive and not well-understood by many
developers. We present the results of a survey where we quizzed developers
about their understanding of the various types of compatibility. 414 developers
responded to our survey. We find that while most programmers are familiar with
the rules of source compatibility, they generally lack knowledge about the
rules of binary and behavioural compatibility. This can be problematic when
organisations switch from integration builds to technologies that require
dynamic linking, such as OSGi. We have assessed the gravity of the problem by
studying how often linkage-related problems are referenced in issue tracking
systems, and find that they are common.

Handwriting recognition is of crucial importance to both Human Computer
Interaction (HCI) and paperwork digitization. In the general field of Optical
Character Recognition (OCR), handwritten Chinese character recognition faces
tremendous challenges due to the enormously large character sets and the
amazing diversity of writing styles. Learning an appropriate distance metric to
measure the difference between data inputs is the foundation of accurate
handwritten character recognition. Existing distance metric learning approaches
either produce unacceptable error rates, or provide little interpretability in
the results. In this paper, we propose an interpretable distance metric
learning approach for handwritten Chinese character recognition. The learned
metric is a linear combination of intelligible base metrics, and thus provides
meaningful insights to ordinary users. Our experimental results on a benchmark
dataset demonstrate the superior efficiency, accuracy and interpretability of
our proposed approach.

Юлия Лазарова Насроченият за 30 март търг за активите на фалиралия ПФК ЦСКА АД със сигурност ще се състои. Това обяви синдикът на дружество Дора Милева за "Дневник"."Има внесен задатък, така че търг ще има", каза Милева след изтичането на крайния срок за завяване на участие. До 17.00 ч. днес желаещите да се включват в наддаването трябваше да внесат 10% от общата сума, на която бяха оценени активите - 1 646 325 лева.Милева отказа да обяви колко ще бъдат участниците в търга. Със сигурност един от кандидатите ще е дружеството "ЦСКА - София", което има отбор в елитната Първа лига и в момента използва стадион "Българска армия" и базата в Панчарево.. БТА съобщи по-рано днес, че единият от собствениците - Гриша Ганчев, е внесъл задатъка и необходимите документи за участие в търга.Засега не е ясно дали другото претендиращо да бъде наследник на ПФК ЦСКА АД дружество - "ЦСКА 1948", ще се включи в наддаването."Няма да даваме такава информация до приключването на търга", заяви пред "Дневник" арх. Ивайло Мишев, който е член на управителния съвет на "ЦСКА 1948".В четвъртък, 30 март, от 13.00 ч. ще започне подаването на офертите, които трябва да бъдат в запечатани пликове. "В 14.00 ч. започвам да отварям предложенията по реда на входирането им, обявявам цените и започваме разглеждането им", обясни Милева. За купувач на активите ще бъде обявен този, който е предложил най-високата цена. Ако повече от един кандидати са предложили най-високата цена, Милева ще определи купувача след незабавен търг с явно наддаване в присъствието на явилите се наддавачи.Победителят в търга трябва да внесе предложената от него сума в седемдневен срок, като внесеният задатък ще му бъде приспаднат.Ако не постъпят оферти или нито една от тях не е редовна, търгът няма да се състои и ще бъде насрочен нов, като цената на активите ще падне с 20% до 1 317 060 лева. Ако и тогава не бъдат продадени, ще се пристъпи към пряко договаряне с кандидат-купувачи.

Mao (pronounced mah-oh) is played with 52 playing cards.  It can be enjoyed as either a single person game against oneself or it can be played between two people.   In this section we describe how to play Mao using the traditional method.    Recruit players. The minimum number of players for Mao is three; however there are usually four or more participants per round.   If you're just starting off learning about Mao then it's best if all of the players sit around a table so that they can easily see what everyone else is doing at any time during the course of their turn.     The dealer has a special... Continue reading

The dealer's job is to shuffle the deck before dealing them into individual hands and also to announce where the next player should go after taking his/her turn.  This means that every time someone takes a turn he/she must move clockwise from wherever he was sitting until reaching another seat occupied by another player whose turn comes up next.   For example, let's assume that Bob sits directly across from Alice while Charlie sits diagonally opposite her/him.  After Bob's turn ends he'll need to move over to the chair that's currently being held by Carol who's seated right behind him/it.  Then once Carol finishes her turn she'll move onto the chair that Bob previously sat down in and continue moving counterclockwise through the circle of chairs until she's back where she started again.   It's important to note here that the dealer does not get to choose whether or not s/he wants to keep his/her position throughout the entirety of the game but rather gets assigned randomly upon beginning the first round of play.   Once you've chosen a new dealer you'll want to reshuffle the deck and begin the process of distributing the cards among yourselves again....

The "Ship of 1000 Cranes" is a large origami model that is fun to fold and makes a great display. 

Step 1:
Start with a square piece of paper. Place the paper face down and fold it along one diagonal, then unfold it.

Step 2:
Fold the lower edges of the square into the centerline.

Step 3:
Turn the paper over so the folds are on the bottom. Fold the lower edges of the square into the centerline.

Step 4:
Fold the lower edge of the square up to the centerline.

Step 5:
Bring the left and right edges of the square together and fold them along the centerline.

Step 6:
Unfold the square, then fold it along the vertical centerline.

Step 7:
Fold the upper edges of the square into the centerline.

Step 8:
Unfold the square, then fold it along the horizontal centerline.

Step 9:
Fold the left and right edges of the square into the centerline.

Step 10:
Take the lower left corner of the square and fold it up to the centerline.

Step 11:
Take the lower right corner of the square and fold it up to the centerline.

Step 12:
Unfold the square, then fold it along the diagonal.

Step 13:
Fold the lower edges of the square into the centerline.

Step 14:
Turn the paper over so the folds are on the bottom. Fold the lower edges of the square into the centerline.

Step 15:
Fold the lower edge of the square up to the centerline.

پاکستان انٹرنیشنل ائیر لائنز (پی ائے اے) نے بنکاک کیلئے کرایوں میں کمی کا اعلان کردیا ہے۔ اس اعلان کے مطابق، پی ائے اے نے بنکاک کیلئے کرایوں میں 10 فیصد تک کمی کردی ہے۔ اس کے علاوہ، پی ائے اے نے بنکاک کیلئے کرایوں کی حدود بھی بڑھا دی ہیں۔

پی ائے اے کے مطابق، بنکاک کیلئے کرایوں کی حدود اب 32 کلوگرام تک بڑھا دی گئی ہیں۔ یہ تبدیلی ہوائی سفر کرنے والوں کے لئے بہترین خبر ہے، جو اب اپنی ضروری اشیاء کو ساتھ لے جاسکتے ہیں۔

پی ائے اے کے اس اعلان کے بعد، بنکاک کیلئے کرایوں کی قیمتوں میں کمی کے ساتھ ساتھ حدود بڑھانے کا فیصلہ بھی سفر کرنے والوں کے لئے بہترین خبر ہے۔

پی ائے اے کے مسئولین نے بتایا کہ یہ تبدیلیاں اس لئے کی گئی ہیں کہ سفر کرنے والوں کو زیادہ سے زیادہ سہولت دی جاسکے۔ انہوں نے کہا کہ پی ائے اے ہمیشہ سے سفر کرنے والوں کی خدمت میں مصروف ہے اور وہ اپنے مسافرین کیلئے مزید سہولیات فراہم کرنے کے لئے مستعد ہیں۔

This paper presents an approach to rapidly and easily build natural language interfaces to databases for new domains, whose performance improves over time based on user feedback. The authors adapt neural sequence models to map utterances directly to SQL with its full expressivity, bypassing any intermediate meaning representations. This approach is deployed online to solicit feedback from real users to flag incorrect queries. The authors then use the crowd to gather annotations for incorrect predictions, which is used to improve their models. The paper concludes with experiments suggesting that this approach can be deployed quickly for any new target domain, as demonstrated by learning a semantic parser for an online academic database from scratch.

Overall, this paper presents an innovative approach to building natural language interfaces to databases for new domains. The authors' use of neural sequence models to map utterances directly to SQL with its full expressivity, and the use of user feedback and crowd annotations to improve the models, is a novel approach that could have broad applications. The experiments and results presented in the paper are well-explained and support the authors' claims. The paper could be improved by providing more detail on the user feedback process, such as the types of user feedback collected and how the feedback is incorporated into the models.

Classification of gamma-ray bursts (GRBs) into groups has been intensively
studied by various statistical tests in previous years. It has been suggested
that there was a distinct group of GRBs, beyond the long and short ones, with
intermediate durations. However, such a group is not securely confirmed yet.
Strangely, concerning the spectral hardness, the observations from the Swift
and RHESSI satellites give different results. For the Swift/BAT database it is
found that the intermediate-duration bursts might well be related to so-called
X-ray flashes (XRFs). On the other hand, for the RHESSI dataset the
intermediate-duration bursts seem to be spectrally too hard to be given by
XRFs. The connection of the intermediate-duration bursts and XRFs for the BATSE
database is not clear as well. The purpose of this article is to check the
relation between XRFs and GRBs for the BATSE and RHESSI databases,
respectively. We use an empirical definition of XRFs introduced by other
authors earlier. For the RHESSI database we also use a transformation between
the detected counts and the fluences based on the simulated detector response
function. The purpose is to compare the hardnesses of GRBs with the definition
of XRFs. There is a 1.3-4.2 % fraction of XRFs in the whole BATSE database. The
vast majority of the BATSE short bursts are not XRFs because only 0.7-5.7 % of
the short bursts can be given by XRFs. However, there is a large uncertainty in
the fraction of XRFs among the intermediate-duration bursts. The fraction of
1-85 % of the BATSE intermediate-duration bursts can be related to XRFs. For
the long bursts this fraction is between 1.0 % and 3.4 %. The uncertainties in
these fractions are large, however it can be claimed that all BATSE
intermediate-duration bursts cannot be given by XRFs. At least 79 % of RHESSI
short bursts, at least 53 % of RHESSI intermediate-duration bursts, and at

Американския телевизионен канал СиБиЕс излъчи програма в която бе дадено място на противозаконните дейности на терористичната организация ФЕТО.В програмата бе отбелязано че ФЕТО изпраща парите, събрани в чартърните училища на движението в САЩ в Турция, за посрещане на нуждите на движението, и че и ФБР е започнало разследване по въпроса.Служител на Държавния департамент е посочил че училищата на ФЕТО укриват пари за да перат мръсни пари и че това представлява организирана престъпност.От 2010 година насам за 136 чартърни училища на ФЕТО в САЩ са били заделени 2,1 милиона долара. В програмата се заявява че трябва да се установи по ясне начин с какви цели са били използвани тези пари.Бившите учители в училищата на ФЕТО в Тексас и Охайо, Ерсин Коркут и Мустафа Еманет са заявили че са били принудени да връщат обратно на движението като дарения близо половината от заплатата която са получавали.Подчертава се че ФБР е по петите на 5 милиона долара дадени на фирмите, учредени от училищни ръководители и проследява процеса за изпращането на тези пари в Турция. В същата програма бившия посланик на САЩ в Анкара Джеймз Джефри подчертава че ФЕТО нанася много голяма вреда на турско-американските отношения и това се отразява негативно на усилията за стабилитет в Близкия изток.Внимание привлече фактът че програмата бе излъчена непосредствено преди посещението на Рекс Тилерсън в Турция.В програматна се заявява че след опита за преврат от 15 юли, въпросът с екстрадирането на лидера на ФЕТО Фетуллах Гюлен е придобил още по-голяма важност от гледна точка на двустранните отношения и припомня че по време на визитата си преди няколко дена във Вашингтон, външният министър на Турция Мевлюд Чавушоглу е предал на министъра на правосъдието на САЩ Джеф Сешън ново досие с доказателства за това че опита за преврат е бил организират от ФЕТО и под ръководството на лидера му Фетуллах Гюлен и че Сешън е обещал да прояви близък интерес към този въпрос.

Do jumps over a soft surface and always wear sneakers or other gym appropriate shoes.;
, Always stretch before jumping and during any other free time. You can even stretch while you watch TV. The following are only a few of many exercises that will help stretch the important muscles used for jumping:

Sitting Straddle: Sit on the floor with your legs out in the shape of a "V" as far as possible. First, lean to your left foot and grab around the foot. You should feel it stretching your side and the back muscles of your thigh. Then, switch to the right side. Finally lean down the middle by pushing your hands forward as far as possible without having the legs or feet turn inwards.






Sitting Toe touch: Sit on the floor with your legs beside you. Point your toes and lean to each side holding for about 10 seconds. Grab your feet if possible and pull your head to your knees. Flex your feet so that your toes point up and stretch side to side again. The stretch will be in the back of your calves (the back of the bottom part of your legs).






Sitting Pike: Sit on the floor with your legs stretched in front of you, knees extended outwards, and your feet touching each other. Stretch in front of you holding for 10 seconds.. Lean forward as far as possible, getting your face close your feet. Then, still keeping your feet together, try holding it longer, or for another 10 seconds.







, As a main part of cheerleading, jumps should be practiced as often as possible. Great jumps make a better cheerleader. Jumps are always done in eight counts. Some popular jumps include the t-jump, spread eagle, hurdler- front and side, shushunova, around the world, double hook, and double 9. Here are a few examples of how to do important jumps:


Toe-Touch. Begin with feet shoulder width apart and hands on your hips. Hold this position for "5,6,7,8". On "1", snap your feet together and put your arms up in a tight V. On "2", swing your arms down so that they cross each other in front of you, and bend your knees. When your arms are fully crossed your knees should be in a deep bend. As you swing your arms out, begin to jump up. Hit the full toe-touch on "3". It should be in the same position as the "toe-touch stretch". Put your arms in a "T". Come down on "4" with hands at your side, feet together, and knees bent. Snap back out to the starting position and hold for "5,6,7,8". This allows you to do many jumps over again.







Pike. Begin in the "prep position" (feet shoulder width apart and hands on your hips). Hold for "5,6,7,8". On "1", turn to either the left or the right and put your arms straight up next to your ears. On "2", swing your arms down and out as far as possible while bending your knees and jump. On "3", hit the jump with the same position as the "pike stretch". With your hands in a fists, reach for your toes. Make sure to bring your feet up as high as possible. Come down and land on "4". Pop back to "prep position" facing forward again for "5,6,7,8".







Herkie-left and right. Begin in the "prep position" for "5,6,7,8". On "1", hit the "V" position with your arms and snap your feet together. On "2", swing down like you would with the "toe-touch". On "3", hit a "T" with your arms. Your right leg should be out as if in a "toe-touch", but your left leg should be bent at the knee so that your foot is behind you as if it is resting on a table. On "4", snap down and land. Pop back to "prep position" and hold "5,6,7,8". You can also do this with your left leg out and your right bent.







, Work the jumps above into various practice routines.


Individual Drill: Start with 3 jumps then build up to 5 or more in a row. For example, do 2 t-jumps followed by 3 toe touches







Forearm Lifts: get into partners, and now get your partner to hold his/her arms in front of them, at a right angle, as if holding a tray, and then place your hands on your partners forearms and jump

Lifts: Get into partners, partner number one should stand behind partner number 2 and hold on to their waist as they jump, assisting the lift of the jump







Team practice: Get into lines and jump one after the other.

, Jumps require muscle strength and flexibility, thus you should always be working to strengthen your muscles.

Do V Snap Ups, V Sits, and leg pulses for good hip flexor strength. Focus on both legs together and individual legs.






Sit on the floor with legs in straddle position. Lift your legs up one at a time and place them back down. Keep your hands between your legs on the floor. Repeat at least ten times on each leg and then again 10 times with both legs together.
Do ab crunches. This will strengthen your abs to further help your jumping technique.






Cheerleading can be dehydrating. Drink plenty of water and get plenty of sleep.







, Put on ankle weights. These will make it harder to do your jumps and will work your leg muscles so when you take them off your jumps are better.


Repeatedly do each jump with weights.
Take off the weights and do the jumps again. Look in a mirror while jumping to see how they improve. Also, when doing toe touches, make sure to have your knees pointed back and your hips open.

Jakarta - Hari Moekti meninggal dunia pada Minggu (24/6) malam. Kabar itu bahkan sempat membuat para sahabatnya kaget dan tak percaya. "Iya (kaget), semalem kita dapet dari WhatsApp aja nggak percaya. 'orang nggak kenapa-kenapa digosipin meninggal'. Baru ane baca detikcom, pas ane lihat bener. Masya Allah," ucap salah seorang sahabatnya, Sholeh Mahmoed Nasution alias ustad Solmed kepada detikHOT, Senin (25/6/2018). Bagaimana tidak, pria yang dulunya dikenal sebagai seorang musisi rock itu tidak pernah mengeluhkan sakit apapun. Hari Moekti bahkan terlihat baik-baik saja di saat mendapat kesempatan untuk mengisi dakwah di mana pun itu. "Nah, itu dia. Terkahir ketemu beliau nggak ngeluh apa apa ya. Sehat, bagus, nggak ada keluhan apa," tutur pria yang akrab disapa Solmed itu. "Makanya pas kemarin denger kejadian beliau meninggal kita kaget juga. Masya Allah. Meninggal kenapa nih. Apa hoax atau bener bener. Soalnya nggak ada informasi awal beliau lagi dirawat atau apa gitu. Bener-bener ngagetin," pungkasnya. Hari Moekti meninggal dunia di usia 61 tahun. Ia menghembuskan nafas terakhirnya di Rumah Sakit Dustira, Cimahi, Jawa Barat pada pukul 20.49 WIB karena sakit stroke. If your engine is in need of a rebuild, it's important to know how to do it properly. This article will walk you through the steps necessary to rebuild an engine.

The first step is to remove the engine from the car. This will require some lifting and pushing, so make sure you have some help. Once the engine is out, you can start taking it apart.

The next step is to remove the cylinder head. This will reveal the internal components of the engine. At this point, you can start taking apart the engine, piece by piece. Be sure to keep all the components in a safe place, so you can find them easily when it comes time to put the engine back together.

Once you have all the components out, it's time to start cleaning them. Use a degreaser and a wire brush to clean all the dirt and grime off the parts. Once they're clean, you can start reassembling the engine.

Be sure to put the engine back together in the correct order. If you don't, it won't work properly. Once the engine is back together, you can put it back in the car and start it up. It's important to be sure that it's running properly before you drive it anywhere.

The tremendous potential of MIMO technology to increase channel capacity and network performance has resulted in its wide adoption for mesh networks. In this study, we investigate the use of "dirty paper coding" (DPC) in Gaussian vector broadcast channels as power allocation for MIMO-based mesh networks. To optimize the performance of the network, we solve a non-convex problem by transforming the original problem and exploiting the channel duality. We present an efficient solution procedure that leverages Lagrangian dual decomposition method, conjugate gradient projection method, cutting-plane method, and subgradient method. Our numerical example significantly improves network performance with a gain of 34.4% compared to using standard power allocation schemes.

They're not the same. The idea behind parallel universes is complex, but the most basic idea is this: At any given time, there's a million things that could happen, but don't actually happen. Scientists who noticed this, started wondering if there was a way to distinguish between all the things that could happen but don't, and the things that actually happening (besides the obvious difference, which is that the things actually happening, happen). Some scientists said we can't tell the difference, because all the things that can happen, actually do happen, just not in our universe, but some other universe. This moved the question to how we ended up in the universe we ended up in, rather than one of the billion parallel universes, to which the most common answer is "Dunno, I'll let you know if I find out".

Dimensions are a completely different thing. Basically, they're ways to describe where things are, or what they're shaped like. The four basic dimensions are height, depth, width, and time. For example, if you take a map, you could describe where you were by using these four dimensions. First, you say how far from the left side of the map you were (width), and how far from the bottom of the map you were (depth). These two numbers will give a single point on the map. Now you describe how high from the ground you were (like, on the second floor, or three kilometers up in an airplane), which gives you height, and finally when you were there. This gets you a unique point. Now, some scientists say that things like gravity can only work the way it works, if there's about a dozen extra dimensions, which none of us can see in any way. These dimensions exist for all parallel universes.

Edit: we don't really *know* any of this is there. Scientists say these things must exist, or otherwise all the math doesn't work out, which means that probably they exist, or just possibly that they've been doing the math slightly wrong. The thing is, we probably won't know *for sure* they've been if they've been doing the math wrong, until somebody discovers a better way, which won't happen if the scientists are actually right.
